"'''Heroes on Both Sides'''" is a 20-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] in the digest-sized [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (graphic novels)|''Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures'' graphic novels]] comic series. ''Heroes on Both Sides'' was published [[April 12]], [[2006]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 5]]''. The comic was written by [[Chris Avellone]], illustrated by [[Stewart McKenny]], lettered by [[Michael David Thomas]], and colored by [[Dan Jackson]].

Set during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], ''Heroes on Both Sides'' tells the story of a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commando]] and a [[Unidentified Viidaav commander|Viidaav commander]] who, despite fighting on opposite sides during the [[Battle of Viidaav]], both tried to stop a detonation sequence that would kill their troops.

==Plot summary==
Above [[Viidaav]], [[Dooku/Legends|Count Dooku]] and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] discussed their plans for the coming trap. Instructing the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] on the ground to activate the detonation sequence, Grievous also mentioned that the [[Viidaav (species)|Viidaav]] colonists should be left to live on in history.

The signal was transmitted to the mining array and was soon detected by both the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] and Viidaav forces. Both rushed to attempt to stop the detonation. Fighting gunships, [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], and security systems, only the two commanders were left in the end. Facing each other with only seconds before detonation, the Viidaav leader was killed, but not before he could shoot the computer. The planet was saved, not by the clone trooper but also by the Viidaav he killed.
